pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 43-52, verified by grep and SQL script analysis
setup_task.sh line 44: INSERT INTO courses statement shows course_name='Advanced Pottery' paired with course_code='ART-303'
setup_task.sh lines 47-49, verified by grep and SQL script analysis. Used as control course for students enrolled in both ART-303 and MATH-101
setup_task.sh lines 72-74: SQL procedure SetupStudents() contains 'WHILE i <= 5 DO' loop, creating exactly 5 students with names PotteryUser1 through PotteryUser5

privileged_info_summary: The cancel_course_enrollments task uses synthetic data with 5 students named PotteryUser1-5. The target course ART-303 ('Advanced Pottery') is a real course section in the OpenSIS database with students enrolled in both the target course and the control course MATH-101 ('Calculus I'). The verifier ensures students are unenrolled from ART-303 (target), the course section remains, students stay active, and control course enrollments remain intact.
